title: Karinjeshwara Temple
tagline: Karinjeshwara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Sri Karinjeshwara Temple (Kannada : ಕಾರಿಂಜೇಶ್ವರ Kārin̄jēśvara) is Hindu Temple, a famous Lord Shiva temple located Karinja in Bantwala Taluk, Kavalamudur Village, Dakshina Kannada,
overview.description: Sri Karinjeshwara Temple (Kannada : ಕಾರಿಂಜೇಶ್ವರ Kārin̄jēśvara) is Hindu Temple, a famous Lord Shiva temple located Karinja in Bantwala Taluk, Kavalamudur Village, Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. This temple is situated on the peak of Karinja Hill, about 1000 feet above sea level in Kodyamale hills, Karinjeshwara. There are about 600 steps to reach this temple. This temple has two parts- one is lord Shiva is on the top of a cliff and the other is for the Goddess Parvati and Lord Ganesha in the middle of the way to the hill.

topPlaces.name: Church of Most Holy Saviour, Agrar
topPlaces.description: The Church of Most Holy Saviour also known as Agrar Church is a historic Roman Catholic Church situated in Bantwal, India. The church was built in 1702, but it is believed that the existence of church was prior to this.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Karinjeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Karinjeshwara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Karinjeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (24 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Karinjeshwara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Karinjeshwara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Karinjeshwara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Karinjeshwara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Karinjeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Church of Most Holy Saviour, Agrar, Kepu, Sajipamuda, Manchi, Phajeer.
